notes.content: All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.
notes.contentHtml:
All transfer applicants must meet the University of California admissions eligibility requirements and the campus’s admission selection criteria. All requirements must be completed by the end of the spring term prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility and admission to UC Santa Cruz, visit the Admissions website.


The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that guarantees your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

notes.content: THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the " Transfer Admission Screening Policy " below. Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion. For more information, including a list of screening majors and non-screening majors , visit the UCSC Admissions website.

THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require completion of specific preparatory coursework and/or a minimum GPA. For details on the specific screening requirements for this major, refer to the "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below.


Additional coursework listed under "Recommended Preparation" is not required for admission but is strongly recommended to support timely degree completion.

notes.content: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website .
notes.contentHtml: Some Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate Higher Level (IBH), and Advanced Level (A-level) exams satisfy UCSC course requirements. UCSC's exam credit policies may be found on the Degree Progress Unit website.

notes.content: EARTH SCIENCES, PLANETARY SCIENCES CONCENTRATION B.S.

The Planetary Sciences concentration is designed to provide students with a quantitative background appropriate for career pathways in the interdisciplinary study of planets and their satellites. The upper-division elective courses can be tailored for students interested in planetary interiors, atmospheres, and/or surfaces. The following transfer screening policy takes effect in fall 2024 and will be mandatory for all transfer students entering UC Santa Cruz fall 2026 and later. Transfer students entering UCSC prior to fall 2026 are encouraged to complete these requirements, but are not required to meet them. Earth Sciences B.S. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer students must complete one of the following courses, or its articulated equivalent, with a grade of C (2.0) or better by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. Strongly Recommended Major Preparation In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below. These courses are prerequisites for upper-division courses required for the major. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree. Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows. Please visit the Earth & Planetary Sciences website to learn more about this major. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Earth Sciences B.S. Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
The Planetary Sciences concentration is designed to provide students with a quantitative background appropriate for career pathways in the interdisciplinary study of planets and their satellites. The upper-division elective courses can be tailored for students interested in planetary interiors, atmospheres, and/or surfaces.



The following transfer screening policy takes effect in fall 2024 and will be mandatory for all transfer students entering UC Santa Cruz fall 2026 and later. Transfer students entering UCSC prior to fall 2026 are encouraged to complete these requirements, but are not required to meet them.

﻿﻿

Earth Sciences B.S. Transfer Admission Screening Policy

Transfer students must complete one of the following courses, or its articulated equivalent, with a grade of C (2.0) or better by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall:


  • MATH 11A – Calculus with Applications
  • MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics


Refer to the "Required Major Preparation Courses" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements.



Strongly Recommended Major Preparation

In addition to the required courses listed above, transfer students are strongly advised to complete the courses listed in the "Recommended Preparation" section below. These courses are prerequisites for upper-division courses required for the major. Students who do not complete these preparatory courses before transferring will likely need additional quarters of enrollment to complete their degree.


Students are strongly encouraged to prioritize required and recommended major preparation courses and may additionally complete courses that fulfill UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before transferring as time allows.


Please visit the Earth & Planetary Sciences website to learn more about this major.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Earth Sciences B.S. Program Statement
